title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition
content:
Charge-Offs & Recoveries
Year-to-date gross charge-offs totaled $169,000.
Losses remain concentrated in Used Vehicle ($95,000) and Unsecured Credit Card ($19,000), which together accounted for the majority of total losses.
Year-to-date recoveries were not disclosed in the source materials for this period,

Allowance & CECL Position
The CECL allowance declined $570, reflecting a -3.4% decrease from the previous quarter. Reserve levels remain aligned with current portfolio risk characteristics, increased charge-off activity, and the decreased trend in overall loan balances. Reserve assumptions continue to incorporate both historical loss experience and forward-looking economic expectations.
Reserve levels address:
- Risk concentrations within Used Vehicle portfolios
- Current delinquency and charge-off trends
- Expected future credit conditions and portfolio performance
Management continues to evaluate CECL assumptions and loss factors to ensure reserve adequacy remains appropriate for the evolving risk profile of the loan portfolio.

Overall Assessment
Loan performance reflects manageable credit risk despite continued contraction in loan balances. Delinquencies remain concentrated within Used Vehicle, while charge-off activity has declined compared with prior periods. The portfolio continues to be supported by strong capital and adequate reserve coverage matched to the actual reserve trend. Management remains focused on disciplined underwriting, proactive collection efforts, and ongoing portfolio monitoring to preserve asset quality while supporting future loan growth and earnings stability.
